titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Roz Chast Papers
titlestmt.author: by Jim Fisher and Rosemary K. J. Davis

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday December 5, 2024 at 23:28

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Funke Literary, LLC on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, and the Jockey Hollow Fund, 2021.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is organized into eight series: I. Cartoons, circa 1970-2020. II. Book projects, circa 1985-2015. III. Other artwork, 1970 - 2019. IV. Correspondence, 1980 - 2019. V. Journals and notebooks, 1969 - 2007. VI. Business papers, 1978 - 2020. VII. Personal papers, 1944 - 2021. VIII. Awards, 1998 - 2019.

bioghist.head: Roz Chast
bioghist.p: Roz Chast (born 1954) is an American cartoonist born in Brooklyn, New York. She is a staff cartoonist for The New Yorker magazine, where she began contributing in 1978. She regularly contributes to other periodicals, including Scientific American , The Village Voice , Redbook , and National Lampoon . Chast has written or illustrated over a dozen books for adults and children, including Unscientific Americans (Dial, 1982), Childproof (Hyperion, 1997), The Party, After You Left (Bloomsbury, 2004), Too Busy Marco (Atheneum, 2010), and Can't We Talk About Something More Pleasant? (Bloomsbury, 2014). She illustrated Steve Martin's The Alphabet from A to Y with Bonus Letter Z! (Flying Dolphin Press, 2007). Chast's work for The New Yorker and other periodicals was collected in Theories of Everything: Selected, Collected, and Health-Inspected Cartoons, 1978-2006 (Bloomsbury, 2008).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Roz Chast Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Roz Chast papers contain correspondence, writings, artwork, printed material, production files, personal papers, photographs, realia, and other papers created by or relating to the life and work of Roz Chast. When possible, Chast's original folder titles have been repurposed as archival description.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Roz Chast Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials largely maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains cartoons created by Roz Chast including drafts, rejected works, prints, and related materials.

did.unittitle: "Pile to go through for various shows" rough drafts
"Sept 86/These are the ones we're using" rough drafts
"Nov 92, resubs and fave stuff" rough drafts
"Bob Eckstine Book of Book Cartoons (original art)"
"Fine Drawings for The Gothamist / Website Ben Yakras"
"Rockwell Museum exhibit binder - Cartoon Memories", correspondence, catalog, notes on exhibit and related ephemera
Notes "Whiskers on roses and raindrops on mittens..."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes business correspondence, cartoons, notes and related materials.

did.unittitle: "Various color and black and white originals"
Photograph titled "Elizabeth Chast" from the Rockwell Museum exhibit "Can't we talk about something more pleasant"
"High quality prints of a few cartoons"

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials largely maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes artwork and related materials created by Roz Chast for multiple book and magazine projects.

did.unittitle: "Brooklyn book materials", research, notes and sketches
"Maria Popova Brian Pickings Website orig. art"
"Originals used in Mort Gerberg's Book about aging"
"What I Hate from A to Z/all alphabet"

title.part: Can't We Talk About Something More Pleasant?

did.unittitle: New Yorker magazine with an excerpt from Can't We Talk About Something More Pleasant?
Original artwork from the Marco books
Original artwork from the Marco Books
Going Into Town: A Love Letter to New York
Samples of mug, pencil case, salt and pepper shakers, graphite pencils, and paper cups with Chast cartoons

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cartoon titles include "What Snowmen Think", "Museum of 1980s Design", "The Jerk With The Cell Phone", "Chutes", "Scents of Tomorrow", and others.

did.unittitle: "Now I Will Never Leave the Dinner Table"

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials largely maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains artwork and related materials created by Roz Chast throughout her career including early works, handmade stamps, lithographs and other prints, a rug, and a number of hand-painted chicken eggs.

did.unittitle: "Material for Now Everyone Really Hates Me"
"Napkin cartoons and post-it notes form Lee Lovery, Bob Mankoff, Mick Stevens, and Michael Crawford"
"Miscellaneous artwork, and wittings not identified", materials include New Yorker tear sheets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Roz Chast work for"The Deck" that was produced by Ink,Inc., New York in 1983 and with designs on every card by New Yorker cartoonists William Steig, 1907-2003 (Hearts), Lee Lorenz, 1932-2022 (Clubs), Roz Chast, 1954- (Diamonds), Arnie Levin, 1938- (Spades), and Jack Ziegler, 1942-2017 (Joker, backs).

did.unittitle: Hand painted chicken eggs with Roz Chast illustrations
"HAND N' HAND" stone lithographs by George Booth, Roz Chast, Arnie Levin, Lee Lorenz, and William Steig - Solo Press, INC

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes one lithograph by each artist with two copies of the lithograph by Roz Chast.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials largely maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence and related materials sent or received by Roz Chast including letters from fans and other cartoonists.

did.unittitle: Letters with cartoons from William Steig and Jack Zeigler

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes cartoons, artwork, and scrapbooks with pasted-down newspaper clippings of strange stories and shocking/odd headlines.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials largely maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: "List of cartoons in the New Yorker"

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials largely maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains materials related to Roz Chast's business dealings including newspaper clippings, correspondence with colleagues at The New Yorker , tear sheets, exhibition materials, and ephemera.

did.unittitle: Awards from the Creative Advertising Club of Detroit

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials are from a working trip taken by Roz Chast and include programs, flyers, and family photographs.

did.unittitle: "Record of sold and unsold through Pam"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tear sheets include "Mother Jones", "Savvy", "National Lampoon", "The Sciences", "INC.", "Seventeen", "Piblishers WeeklY", "The New Yorker" and other publications.

did.unittitle: Ephemera, exhibition cards, and related materials
"Laminated Star Tribune page, Drawing from Life"
Exhibit room layout model labeled "Rankine/Lucas-NOV 103" with build instructions

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials largely maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains materials from Roz Chast's early life including childhood artwork, drawings, photographs. Also included are artwork and other materials from Chast's time at the Rhode Island School of Design.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials were included in the exhibit "The Masters Series: Roz Chast at School of Visual Arts in New York City", November 2018.

did.unittitle: Rhode Island School of Design papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes prints and a pen and ink drawing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscription on the back of the print reads "Dear Dad, Happy Birthday and all that etc. Love Roz"

did.unittitle: Note from mother "How you got your name"

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials largely maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains awards and recognitiion received by Roz Chast throughout her career including a finalist certificate from the Thurber Awards in 2015, which is the first year where the entire slate of Thurber Award finalists were all women.

did.unittitle: "American Academy of Arts and Sciences, and photograph "
"JTS honorary doctorate letter, JTS commencement programs "
"Hamilton College commencement program, Hamilton College, letter of congratulations "
"Lesley University, honorary doctorate, Lesley University, letter of congratulations"
